clinical.presenting_complaints: Patient presented to emergency department with acute onset severe abdominal pain in right lower quadrant for past 12 hours. Pain started periumbilically and migrated to right iliac fossa. Associated with nausea, vomiting (3 episodes), and fever (101 F). Pain aggravated by movement and coughing.
clinical.initial_assessment: Physical Examination: Temperature 101.2 F, Pulse 110/min, BP 120/80 mmHg. Abdomen showed tenderness and guarding in right iliac fossa, positive McBurney's point tenderness, positive Rovsing's sign, and rebound tenderness. USG Abdomen (09/10/2025): Dilated appendix 9mm diameter with surrounding fluid collection suggestive of perforation and localized peritonitis. Blood Investigations (09/10/2025): TLC 18,500/cumm (elevated), Neutrophils 85%, CRP 45 mg/L (elevated). Serum electrolytes and renal function tests within normal limits. Blood sugar random 110 mg/dL.
clinical.significant_past_history: No significant past medical history. No chronic illnesses. No known drug allergies. No previous surgeries.
clinical.primary_diagnosis_at_admission: Acute Appendicitis with Perforation and Localized Peritonitis (ICD-10: K35.32)
clinical.final_diagnosis_at_discharge: Post-operative status following emergency laparoscopic appendectomy with peritoneal lavage and drain placement for perforated appendicitis with localized peritonitis (ICD-10: K35.32)
clinical.key_investigations: USG Abdomen, Blood Investigations (TLC, Neutrophils, CRP, Serum electrolytes, renal function tests, Blood sugar random)
clinical.investigation_findings: USG Abdomen (09/10/2025): Dilated appendix 9mm diameter with surrounding fluid collection suggestive of perforation and localized peritonitis. Blood Investigations (09/10/2025): TLC 18,500/cumm (elevated), Neutrophils 85%, CRP 45 mg/L (elevated). Serum electrolytes and renal function tests within normal limits. Blood sugar random 110 mg/dL.
clinical.treatment_given: Day 0 (10/10/2025): Post-op patient stable. IV antibiotics initiated — Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g BD and Inj. Metronidazole 500mg TDS. IV fluids maintained. NPO status. Vitals stable. Day 1 (11/10/2025): Patient afebrile. Started on oral fluids. Drain output sero-sanguinous, reducing. Ambulation encouraged. Day 2 (12/10/2025): Drain removed — output minimal and clear. Started on soft diet. Wound inspection — clean and dry. Day 3 (13/10/2025): Switched to oral antibiotics. Tolerating regular diet. No abdominal tenderness on examination. Day 4 (14/10/2025): Patient comfortable, afebrile, tolerating regular diet, all surgical wounds clean and dry, ambulatory without assistance. Declared medically fit for discharge.
clinical.operative_findings: Standard 3-port laparoscopic technique used. On entry, perforated appendix identified with a localized abscess of approximately 30 mL purulent collection in the right iliac fossa. Appendectomy performed with endoloop ligation of appendicular base. Thorough peritoneal lavage performed with warm normal saline until clear. A surgical drain placed in the right iliac fossa. No intraoperative complications. Estimated blood loss: 50 mL. No implants used.
clinical.final_procedure_performed: Emergency Laparoscopic Appendectomy with Peritoneal Lavage and Drain Placement
clinical.complications: Intraoperative finding of perforated appendix with localized abscess (30 mL) — an unanticipated pathology that required modified surgical approach with peritoneal lavage and drain placement, and extended IV antibiotic coverage. No post-operative complications. Wound healing satisfactory.
clinical.advice_on_discharge: Follow-up Schedule: Day 7 post-surgery (17/10/2025) — wound check and suture removal at nearest PHC or surgical OPD. Day 14 post-surgery (24/10/2025) — final post-operative assessment. Activity Restrictions: Avoid heavy lifting (more than 5 kg) for 2 weeks. Avoid strenuous exercise for 3 weeks. Warning Signs — Return Immediately If: Fever above 100 F, severe abdominal pain, redness or discharge from wounds, or persistent vomiting.

Apollo Hospitals Chennai

PATIENT CLINICAL RECORD — INPATIENT DISCHARGE
Record No: APL/CHN/IPD/2025/010456 Date of Issue: 14/10/2025
SECTION 1: PATIENT AND ADMISSION INFORMATION
Patient Name: Ramesh Kumar IPD Number: IPD/SG/2025/010456
Age / Gender: 28 Years / Male PMJAY ID: PMJAY-MH-2024-P004
Date of Birth: 15/03/1997 PMJAY Case ID: IRIS-CASE1-20251010
Address: 45, MG Road, Bangalore – 560001 Contact: +91-9876543210
Date of Admission: 10/10/2025 at 02:30 hrs Date of Discharge: 14/10/2025 at 11:00 hrs
Duration of Stay: 4 Days Type of Admission: Emergency
Ward Category: General Ward PM-JAY Package Booked:SG018A
SECTION 2: TREATING CONSULTANT
Consultant Name: Dr. Suresh Patel Registration No: KMC-12345-2010
Qualification: MBBS, MS (General Surgery) Specialty: General Surgery
Contact: 9845678901 Department: General Surgery, Apollo Hospitals Chennai
SECTION 3: CLINICAL SUMMARY
Presenting Complaints:
Patient presented to emergency department with acute onset severe abdominal pain in right lower quadrant for past 12 hours. Pain
started periumbilically and migrated to right iliac fossa. Associated with nausea, vomiting (3 episodes), and fever (101 F). Pain
aggravated by movement and coughing.
Past Medical and Surgical History:
No significant past medical history. No chronic illnesses. No known drug allergies. No previous surgeries.
Pre-operative Examination and Investigations:
Physical Examination: Temperature 101.2 F, Pulse 110/min, BP 120/80 mmHg. Abdomen showed tenderness and guarding in right
iliac fossa, positive McBurney's point tenderness, positive Rovsing's sign, and rebound tenderness. USG Abdomen (09/10/2025):
Dilated appendix 9mm diameter with surrounding fluid collection suggestive of perforation and localized peritonitis. Blood
Investigations (09/10/2025): TLC 18,500/cumm (elevated), Neutrophils 85%, CRP 45 mg/L (elevated). Serum electrolytes and renal
function tests within normal limits. Blood sugar random 110 mg/dL.
SECTION 4: DIAGNOSIS
Provisional Diagnosis Acute Appendicitis with Perforation and Localized Peritonitis (ICD-10: K35.32)
(at admission):
Final Diagnosis Post-operative status following emergency laparoscopic appendectomy with peritoneal lavage and drain placement for perforated appendicitis with localized peritonitis (ICD-10: K35.32)
(at discharge):
SECTION 5: PROCEDURE PERFORMED
Procedure Name: Emergency Laparoscopic Appendectomy with Peritoneal Lavage and Drain Placement
PM-JAY Package Code: SG018A — Appendicular Perforation
Date and Time of Surgery: 10/10/2025 at 08:00 hrs
Duration: 75 minutes
Anaesthesia: General Anaesthesia
Anaesthetist: Dr. Priya Sharma
Operative Findings and Technique:
Standard 3-port laparoscopic technique used. On entry, perforated appendix identified with a localized abscess of approximately 30
mL purulent collection in the right iliac fossa. Appendectomy performed with endoloop ligation of appendicular base. Thorough
peritoneal lavage performed with warm normal saline until clear. A surgical drain placed in the right iliac fossa. No intraoperative
complications. Estimated blood loss: 50 mL. No implants used.
SECTION 6: POST-OPERATIVE COURSE AND TREATMENT
Day 0 (10/10/2025): Post-op patient stable. IV antibiotics initiated — Inj. Ceftriaxone 1g BD and Inj. Metronidazole 500mg TDS. IV
fluids maintained. NPO status. Vitals stable. Day 1 (11/10/2025): Patient afebrile. Started on oral fluids. Drain output
sero-sanguinous, reducing. Ambulation encouraged. Day 2 (12/10/2025): Drain removed — output minimal and clear. Started on soft
diet. Wound inspection — clean and dry. Day 3 (13/10/2025): Switched to oral antibiotics. Tolerating regular diet. No abdominal
tenderness on examination. Day 4 (14/10/2025): Patient comfortable, afebrile, tolerating regular diet, all surgical wounds clean and
dry, ambulatory without assistance. Declared medically fit for discharge.
SECTION 7: COMPLICATIONS
Intraoperative finding of perforated appendix with localized abscess (30 mL) — an unanticipated pathology that required modified
surgical approach with peritoneal lavage and drain placement, and extended IV antibiotic coverage. No post-operative complications.
Wound healing satisfactory.
SECTION 8: CONDITION AT DISCHARGE
Discharge Status: Recovered Discharge Condition: Stable
Actual LOS: 4 Days (10/10/2025 to 14/10/2025) Ward Category: General Ward (Ward)
Bed Breakdown: Ward: 4 days | HDU: 0 | ICU: 0 | ICU-Vent: 0 Implants Used: None
Complications: None post-operative Patient Amount Collected:Rs. 11,500 (non-PM-JAY items)
SECTION 9: DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS
1. Tab. Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 625mg — 1 tablet three times daily for 5 days
2. Tab. Metronidazole 400mg — 1 tablet three times daily for 5 days
3. Tab. Paracetamol 500mg — 1-2 tablets as needed for pain for 7 days
4. Tab. Pantoprazole 40mg — 1 tablet once daily before breakfast for 7 days
SECTION 10: FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS
Follow-up Schedule: Day 7 post-surgery (17/10/2025) — wound check and suture removal at nearest PHC or surgical OPD. Day 14
post-surgery (24/10/2025) — final post-operative assessment.
Activity Restrictions: Avoid heavy lifting (more than 5 kg) for 2 weeks. Avoid strenuous exercise for 3 weeks.
Warning Signs — Return Immediately If: Fever above 100 F, severe abdominal pain, redness or discharge from wounds, or
persistent vomiting.
PM-JAY Claim Reference: IRIS-CASE1-20251010 | Package: SG018A — Appendicular Perforation
SECTION 11: DOCUMENTS SUBMITTED WITH CLAIM

SECTION 12: DECLARATIONS AND SIGNATURES
I certify that the patient was under my care, the treatment rendered was medically necessary, and all information provided in this
record is true and correct to the best of my knowledge. The extended hospital stay was medically warranted due to intraoperative
findings of perforated appendicitis with peritonitis requiring IV antibiotic therapy.
